Crucially, in adherence to Protocol 7, single-character words or roots, including common functional morphemes like 'ና' (and), 'በ' (in/by), and the name fragment 'ሞ', were strictly discarded from the final dataset.

The input batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. Several words containing the conjunction 'እና' (and) or the emphatic particles 'ም' (also/even) and 'ማ' (indeed) were split according to PROTOCOL 1 (THE SPLITTER). The single-character particles 'ም' and 'ማ' were subsequently discarded as per PROTOCOL 7 (THE FILTER) because Amharic words must be at least 2 characters long. All remaining words were successfully corrected (PROTOCOL 3), lemmatized to their global citation forms (PROTOCOL 4), translated (PROTOCOL 5), and tagged with their Part of Speech (PROTOCOL 6). No gibberish or unfixable OCR errors were detected, thus PROTOCOL 2 (THE JUDGE) did not result in further discards beyond the single-character particles.

summary:
The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words required splitting to isolate individual lexical units and affixes. For instance, 'ቫንፒርሲስሮቢን' was split into 'ቫንፒር', 'ሲስ', and 'ሮቢን', recognizing them as distinct proper nouns. Similarly, 'እርቁንና' became 'እርቁን' and 'እና', and 'ጋርሺያንም' became 'ጋርሺያ' and 'ንም'.

Crucially, Protocol 7, which mandates discarding any word or root consisting of only a single character, led to the removal of several elements. The preposition 'በ' (from 'በወፍበረርጋሼ' and 'በሶራምባ'), the particle 'ም' (from 'በጥንትም'), and the particle 'ማ' (from 'የታደለማ') were all discarded as they are single-character units. This strict adherence ensures the dataset only contains words meeting the minimum length requirement.

Scholarly Guesses were made for transliterated proper nouns like 'ጽርሃርያም', 'ሶራምባ', 'ሜርሰዲስ', and 'ፊያ', which were kept as single units. The word 'የዱቄ' was lemmatized to 'ዱቄት', assuming the final 'ት' was omitted due to grammatical context. The phrase 'በወፍበረርጋሼ' was analyzed by splitting it into its constituent parts 'ወፍ', 'በረር', and 'ጋሼ' (with 'በ' discarded), acknowledging 'ወፍ በረር' as an idiomatic expression for 'quickly'. Part of Speech tagging was applied contextually, differentiating between simple verbs, relative verbs, and converbs. All words were assigned a default importance of 5.

Several words required splitting: 'ያረጋገጠችውና' into 'ያረጋገጠችው' and 'ና'; 'የብቃትንና' into 'የብቃትን' and 'ና'; 'ከሆዳቸውና' into 'ከሆዳቸው' and 'ና'; 'እያሰላሰልኩኝተስፈኛውን' into 'እያሰላሰልኩኝ' and 'ተስፈኛውን'; 'እርግዝናዋም' into 'እርግዝናዋ' and 'ም'; and 'እንደማያደርግበትና' into 'እንደማያደርግበት' and 'ና'. Two words were discarded due to being unfixable gibberish or severe typos: 'እነተምትም' (specifically 'ተምትም') and 'ከትንት'. Visual OCR corrections were applied to 'ማበራታቻ' which was corrected to 'ማበረታቻ' (incentive) and 'በነፍስት' which was corrected to 'በነፍስ' (soul/spirit). 'ተኮላዊያን' was treated as a scholarly guess, inferring its root 'ተኮላ' and meaning 'people of/related to ተኮላ', acknowledging its potential rarity or specific context. All resulting words and roots are at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint.

The following words were split from their original input due to common Amharic morphological merges: 'ቃጠሎውና' into 'ቃጠሎው', 'ማዞሪያሽም' into 'ማዞሪያሽ', 'የከተታትንና' into 'የከተታትን', 'የተተካውና' into 'የተተካው', 'ገዢዎችስ' into 'ገዢዎች', 'እየተነገራቸውና' into 'እየተነገራቸው', 'ለግላዊና' into 'ለግላዊ', and 'የግልበጣና' into 'የግልበጣ'. The word 'ለዚህመም' was corrected to 'ለዚህም', as the trailing 'ም' was identified as an OCR error or redundant emphatic particle. A scholarly guess was made for 'ባኮች' to be the proper noun 'Bach', given the presence of other proper nouns like 'ኤለን' (Ellen), 'ሚቼል' (Michelle), and 'ሩፔርት' (Rupert) in the batch. No words were discarded for being gibberish or single characters. All words were successfully lemmatized to their global citation forms and assigned appropriate parts of speech and synonyms.
